title: PS-69955: Reduce application to SQL Server calls of SYSUTCDATETIME()
body:
Implement process-wide DB UTC offset caching by SQL data source so repeated timestamp requests do not execute SYSUTCDATETIME() on every call. Add configurable refresh interval (default 60 minutes), stale-entry eviction, and single-flight refresh so concurrent refreshes issue one calibration query per key.

Harden fallback behavior by validating calibration values, throttling repeated failures, and handling null/empty/unparseable data sources. Keep TestRunner/ServerMonitor on uncached calibration, and add focused unit tests for concurrency, eviction bounds, unknown-key paths, and calibration-key branches.
author: jimblythe-trimble

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Implements a process-wide cache for the local-UTC ↔ SQL Server `SYSUTCDATETIME()` tick offset, keyed by SQL data source, to avoid re-running the calibration query on every `GetDBUtcNow()` call. This integrates into `CachedDataProvider.GetDBUtcNow()` (with TestRunner/ServerMonitor bypass) and adds configuration + unit test coverage for concurrency, refresh, eviction, and fallback paths.

**Changes:**
- Added `DbUtcClockCache` to single-flight and cache DB UTC calibration per SQL data source, with refresh + eviction and failure throttling.
- Updated `CachedDataProvider.GetDBUtcNow()` to use the cached offset (or uncached path for TestRunner/ServerMonitor) and to fall back safely to local UTC.
- Added configuration plumbing for `DbUtcNowCacheRefreshMinutes` and unit tests covering the new behavior.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| Core/Web Layer/WebUI/Web.config | Documents the new optional `DbUtcNowCacheRefreshMinutes` setting. |
| Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/ServerComponents/DbUtcClockCache.cs | Introduces the process-wide per-data-source offset cache with refresh, eviction, and throttling. |
| Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/ServerComponents/CachedDataProvider.cs | Switches `GetDBUtcNow()` to use `DbUtcClockCache` with safe fallback + warning throttling. |
| Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/Configuration.cs | Adds parsing/storage for the refresh interval setting (defaulting to 60 minutes). |
| Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/ServerComponents/DBHelper.cs | Adds documentation clarifying `AdvanceWhenRequestedToNow()` behavior with the new offset caching. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.UnitTests/DataAccess/ServerComponents/DbUtcClockCacheTests.cs | Adds unit tests for cache keying, concurrency single-flight, refresh, eviction, and throttling. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.UnitTests/DataAccess/ServerComponents/CachedDataProviderGetDBUtcNowTests.cs | Adds unit tests for `GetDBUtcNow()` behavior across reader-open, TestRunner bypass, and cached behavior. |
</details>
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

reviews.body: Looks good to me, with the caveat that I'm new around this codebase!
reviews.author: davesanderling

reviews.body: Hi Please don't merge just yet. This branch is currently frozen for a release.
reviews.author: AdamCollins

reviews.body: @jimblythe-trimble The changes in this PR are incorrect and unnecessary. The reason for the number of calls in the project list is that we must open a connection to each portfolio database. Each database connection stands on its own merits and performs its own caching. I am going to close the ticket as will not implement.
reviews.author: kcostenbader

review_comments.body: When evicting a stale cache entry, the corresponding failure-throttle entry in s_LastCalibrationFailureUtcTicks is left behind indefinitely. In long-running processes with many distinct data sources that fail at least once, this dictionary can grow without bound even though the main cache entries are being evicted.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: This newly introduced const name does not follow the file’s existing constant naming convention (e.g., STORED_PROC, SUBAREA) and the project guideline that const identifiers use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE. Renaming it will require updating all references to keep the file compiling.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Cached entries keyed by the connection DataSource (server name) are expected to be minimal and controlled by DevOps to not include frequent failure conditions. Failure handling and cache eviction primarily included for edge case validation through unit tests.
review_comments.author: jimblythe-trimble

issue_comments.body:
Thanks Karl
My perspective is that the UTC in SQL Server is server-instance based and
does not vary by database instance. This was the reasoning behind using the
DataSource (server) as the cache key.

Ultimately, this was mostly an exercise in applying a code change, but also
hit me from prior experience to reduce app to SQL hits leaving more
capacity for real data requests.

issue_comments.author: jimblythe-trimble

issue_comments.body: @jimblythe-trimble I definitely appreciate you taking the initiative on this, but the context that you are missing is that in production there are at least 8 different SQL instances in order to spread the load.
issue_comments.author: kcostenbader

issue_comments.body: I like the approach @jimblythe-trimble! I would be really interested in seeing what your profiling results were before and after.
issue_comments.author: AdamCollins

issue_comments.body: @AdamCollins Why? SQL server is not under any sort of real load (~50% utilization) and the observed issue only happens when multiple DBs are opened and queried in the projects page. Otherwise, the instance of DBHelper that is opened maintains a cached version. While the approach is interesting, it adds a complication that affects 99.9+% of operations for a single page.
issue_comments.author: kcostenbader

issue_comments.body:
I truly appreciate the feedback and most value the secondary response of **"in production there are at least 8 different SQL instances"** as I know I do not yet have a mental image of the deployed landscape.
_Along the lines of furthering my education (not trying to revive the PR, just probing a little deeper for better understanding), I hope you do not mind me continuing this thread._

I had noted that IgnitionMaster's dbo.Organizations table permitted the <...>_ProjectSight instance to be on different servers.
**Is this the scale out or is there something more dynamic in play?**
I did account for the ConnectionString being modified (e.g. in ConfigurePortfolioConnectionStringTemplates), so the logic using Connecton.DataSource properly keyed by the modified server name. But if there is a dynamic routing unknown to the app, then discovery of server name would outweigh the SQL query.

__Flushing out other thoughts:__
- "profiling results were before and after" - locally (laptop app to laptop SQL), this is 1-2ms. I have seen deployed environments under load where it was 15-50ms, but production ProjectSight is likely ~5ms. Even with ~8 SQL Servers, the static dictionary store in the app instances was desired to reduce network traffic and SQL CPU usage (no actual data involved). Minimal savings, but the shift to more and more microservices makes it a higher percentage of each hit.
- "SQL server is not under any sort of real load" = valuable insight
- "observed issue..." proposed change was not noted as an issue, just a potential optimization from a new employee discovering the code base :)
- "instance of DBHelper ... maintains a cached version" - yes, valuable caching noted right away, but within each endpoint use
- "complication that affects 99.9+% of operations for a single page" - again not noted as any sort of issue or for a specific page. Intended as a systemic improvement as many DBHelper uses fill the UTC offset value supporting numerous calls for WhenRequested.

**_Thanks again for supporting my learning growth._**
issue_comments.author: jimblythe-trimble

+    <!-- Minutes between SYSUTCDATETIME() calibrations per SQL data source (default 60 if omitted) -->

   </DataAccessConfiguration>
   <system.web>
     <caching>
